author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And it was at Jerusalem, the Feast of the dedication, and it was winter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon’s porch.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then came the Jews round about Him, and said unto Him, How long dost thou make us to doubt?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Every virtue is a good thing, but most of all gentleness and meekness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This showeth us men; this maketh us to differ from wild beasts; this fitteth us to vie with Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ continually expendeth many words about this virtue, bidding us be meek and gentle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor doth He merely expend words about it, but also teacheth it by His actions; at one time buffeted and bearing it, at another reproached and plotted against; yet again coming to those who plotted against Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For those men who had called Him a demoniac, and a Samaritan and who had often desired to kill Him, and had cast stones at Him, the same surrounded and asked Him, “Art thou the Christ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not even in this case did He reject them after so many and so great plots against Him, but answered them with great gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But it is necessary rather to enquire into the whole passage from the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It was,” It saith, “at Jerusalem, the Feast of the dedication, and it was winter.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This feast was a great and national one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they celebrated with great zeal the day on which the Temple was rebuilt, on their return from their long captivity in Persia.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At this feast Christ also was present, for henceforth He continually abode in Judæa, because the Passion was nigh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then came the Jews round about Him, and said, How long dost thou make us to doubt?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He did not reply, “What enquire ye of Me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Often have ye called Me demoniac, madman, and Samaritan, and have deemed me an enemy of God, and a deceiver, and ye said but now, Thou bearest witness of thyself, thy witness is not true; how is it then that ye seek and desire to learn from Me, whose witness ye reject?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He said nothing of the kind, although He knew that the intention with which they made the enquiry was evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For their surrounding Him and saying, “How long dost thou make us to doubt?” seemed to proceed from a certain longing and desire of learning, but the intention with which they asked the question was corrupt and deceitful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since His works admitted not of their slander and insolence, while they might attack His sayings by finding out in them a sense other than that in which they were spoken, they continually proposed questions, desiring to silence Him by means of His sayings; and when they could find no fault with His works, they wished to find a handle in His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore they said, “Tell us”; yet He had often told them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He said to the woman of Samaria, “I Am that speak unto thee” ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 26 ); and to the blind man, “Thou hast both seen Him, and it is He that talketh with thee.” ( c. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He had told them also, if not in the same, at least in other words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And indeed, had they been wise, and had they desired to enquire aright, it remained for them to confess Him by words, since by works He had often proved the point in question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now observe their perverse and disputations temper.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He addresseth them, and instructeth them by His words, they say, “What sign showest thou us?” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when He giveth them proofs by His works, they say to Him, “Art thou the Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Tell us plainly”; when the works cry aloud, they seek words, and when the words teach, then they betake themselves to works, ever setting themselves to the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But that they enquired not for the sake of learning, the end showed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Him whom they deemed to be so worthy of credit, as to receive His witness of Himself, when He had spoken a few words they straightway stoned; so that their very surrounding and pressing upon Him was done with ill intent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the mode of questioning was full of much hatred.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Tell us plainly, Art thou the Christ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He spake all things openly, being ever present at their feasts, and in secret He said nothing; but they brought forward words of deceit, “How long dost thou make us to doubt?” in order that having drawn Him out, they might again find some handle against Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that in every case they questioned Him not in order to learn, but to find fault with His words, is clear, not from this passage only, but from many others also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since when they came to Him and asked, “Is it lawful to give tribute unto Cæsar or not?” ( Matt. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ), when they spake about putting away a wife ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ), when they enquired about her who, they said, had had seven husbands ( Matt. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 23 ), they were convicted of bringing their questions to Him, not from desire of learning, but from an evil intention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But there He rebuked them, saying, “Why tempt ye Me, ye hypocrites?” showing that He knew their secret thoughts, while here He said nothing of the kind; teaching us not always to rebuke those who plot against us, but to bear many things with meekness and gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then it was a sign of folly, when the works proclaimed Him aloud, to seek the witness of words, hear how He answereth them, at once hinting to them that they made these enquiries superfluously, and not for the sake of learning, and at the same time showing that He uttered a voice plainer than that by words, namely, that by works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I told you often,” He saith, “and ye believe not: the works that I do in My Father’s Name, they are they that bear witness of Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A remark which the more tolerable among them continually made to one another; “A man that is a sinner cannot do such miracles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “A devil cannot open the eyes of the blind”: and, “No man can do such miracles except God be with him.” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And beholding the miracles that He did, they said, “Is not this the Christ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Others said, “When Christ cometh, will He do greater miracles than those which this Man hath done?” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And these very persons as many as then desired to believe on Him, saying, “What sign showest thou us, that we may see, and believe thee?” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When then they who had not been persuaded by such great works, pretended that they should be persuaded by a bare word, He rebuketh their wickedness, saying, “If ye believe not My works, how will ye believe My words? so that your questioning is superfluous.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” He saith, “I told you, and ye believe not, because ye are not of My sheep.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For I on My part have fulfilled all that it behooved a Shepherd to do, and if ye follow Me not, it is not because I am not a Shepherd, but because ye are not My sheep.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For My sheep hear My voice, and follow Me; and I give unto them eternal life ; neither can any man pluck them out of My hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Father, which gave them Me, is greater than all, and no man is able to pluck them out of My Father’s hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I and the Father are One.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how in renouncing He exciteth them to follow Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye hear Me not,” He saith, “for neither are ye sheep, but they who follow, these are of the flock.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He said, that they might strive to become sheep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then by mentioning what they should obtain, He maketh these men jealous, so as to rouse them, and cause them to desire such things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it through the power of the Father that no man plucketh them away, and hast thou no strength, but art too weak to guard them?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in order that thou mayest learn that the expression, “The Father which gave them to Me,” is used on their account, that they might not again call Him an enemy of God, therefore, after asserting that, “No man plucketh them out of My hand,” He proceedeth to show, that His hand and the Father’s is One.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since had not this been so, it would have been natural for Him to say, “The Father which gave them to Me is greater than all, and no man can pluck them out of My hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He said not so, but, “out of My Father’s hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then that thou mayest not suppose that He indeed is weak, but that the sheep are in safety through the power of the Father, He addeth, “I and the Father are One.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said “I did not assert that on account of the Father no man plucketh them away, as though I were too weak to keep the sheep.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I and the Father are One.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Speaking here with reference to Power, for concerning this was all His discourse; and if the power be the same, it is clear that the Essence is also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when the Jews used ten thousand means, plotting and casting men out of their synagogues, He telleth them that all their contrivances are useless and vain; “For the sheep are in My Father’s hand”; as the Prophet saith, “Upon My hand I have pictured thy walls.” ( Isa. xlix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then to show that the hand is One, He sometimes saith that it is His own, sometimes the Father’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when thou hearest the word “hand,” do not understand anything material, but the power, the authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, if it was on this account that no one could pluck away the sheep, because the Father gave Him power, it would have been superfluous to say what follows, “I and the Father are One.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since were He inferior to Him, this would have been a very daring saying, for it declares nothing else than an equality of power; of which the Jews were conscious, and took up stones to cast at Him. ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet not even so did He remove this opinion and suspicion; though if their suspicion were erroneous, He ought to have set them right, and to have said, “Wherefore do ye these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I spake not thus to testify that my power and the Father’s are equal”; but now He doth quite the contrary, and confirmeth their suspicion, and clencheth it, and that too when they were exasperated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He maketh no excuse for what had been said, as though it had been said ill, but rebuketh them for not entertaining a right opinion concerning Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For a good work we stone thee not, but for blasphemy; and because that thou being a man makest thyself God”; hear His answer; “If the Scripture called them gods unto whom the word of God came, how say ye that I blaspheme, because I said, I am the Son of God?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is of this kind: “If those who have received this honor by grace, are not found fault with for calling themselves gods, how can He who hath this by nature deserve to be rebuked?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He spake not so, but proved it at a later time, having first relaxed and yielded somewhat in His discourse, and said, “Whom the Father hath sanctified and sent.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when He had softened their anger, He bringeth forward the plain assertion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a while, that His speech might be received, He spoke in a humbler strain, but afterwards He raised it higher, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If I do not the works of My Father, believe Me not; but if I do, though ye believe not Me, believe the works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He proveth what I said, that He is in nothing inferior to the Father, but in every way equal to Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since it was impossible to see His Essence, from the equality and sameness of the works He affordeth a proof of unvaryingness as to Power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what, tell me, shall we believe?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That I am in the Father, and the Father in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For I am nothing other than what the Father is, yet still Son; He nothing other than what I am, yet still Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if any man know Me, he knoweth the Father, and if he knoweth the Father, he hath learnt also the Son.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now were the power inferior, then also what relateth to the knowledge would be false, for it is not possible to become acquainted with one substance or power by means of another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore they sought again to take Him, but He escaped out of their hands, and went away again beyond Jordan, into the place where John at first baptized.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And many resorted unto Him, and said, John did no miracle, but all things that John spake of this man were true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When He hath uttered anything great and sublime, He quickly retireth, giving way to their anger, so that the passion may abate and cease through His absence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And thus He acted at that time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore doth the Evangelist mention the place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest learn that He went there to remind them of the things there done and said by John, and of his testimony; at least when they came there, they straightway remembered John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also they said, “John indeed did no miracle,” since how did it follow that they should add this, unless the place had brought the Baptist to their memory, and they had come to remember his testimony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe how they form incontrovertible syllogisms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “John indeed did no miracle,” “but this man doth,” saith some one; “hence therefore his superiority is shown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If therefore men believed him who did no miracles, much more must they believe this man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then, since it was John who bore the witness, lest his having done no miracle might seem to prove him unworthy of being a witness, they added, “Yet if he did no miracle, still he spake all things truly concerning this man”; no longer proving Christ to be trustworthy by means of John, but John to be so by what Christ had done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Many therefore believed on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There were many things that attracted them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They remembered the words which John had spoken, calling Christ “mightier than himself,” and “light,” and “life,” and “truth,” and all the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They remembered the Voice which came down from heaven, and the Spirit which appeared in the shape of a dove, and pointed Him out to all; and with this they recollected the demonstration afforded by the miracles, looking to which they were for the future established.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For,” saith some one, “if it was right that we should believe John, much more ought we to believe this man; if him without miracles, much more this man, who besides the testimony of John, hath also the proof from miracles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how much the abiding in this place, and the being freed from the presence of evil men, profited them? wherefore Jesus continually leadeth and draweth them away from the company of those persons; as also He seemeth to have done under the old Covenant, forming and ordering the Jews in all points, in the desert, at a distance from the Egyptians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He now adviseth us also to do, bidding us avoid public places, and tumults, and disturbances, and pray peacefully in the chamber.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the vessel which is free from confusion, sails with a fair wind, and the soul which is separated from worldly matters rests in harbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore women ought to have more true wisdom than men, because they are for the most part riveted to keeping at home.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So, for instance, Jacob was a plain man, because he dwelt at home, and was free from the bustle of public life; for not without a cause hath Scripture put this, when It saith, “dwelling in a house.” ( Gen. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some woman, “even in a house there is great confusion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yes, when thou wilt have it so, and bringest about thyself a crowd of cares.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the man who spends his time in the midst of the market-places and courts of justice is overwhelmed, as if by waves, by external troubles; but the women who sits in her house as in some school of true wisdom, and collects her thoughts within herself, will be enabled to apply herself to prayers, and readings, and other heavenly wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as they who dwell in deserts have none to disturb them, so she being continually within can enjoy a perpetual calm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor even if at any time she need to go forth, is there then any cause for confusion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the necessary occasions for a women to leave her house are, either for the purpose of coming hither, or when the body need to be cleansed in the bath; but for the most part she sits at home, and it is possible for her both to be herself truly wise, and receiving her husband when agitated to calm and compose him, to abate the excess and fierceness of his thoughts, and so to send him forth again, having put off all the mischiefs which he collected from the market-place, and carrying with him whatever good he learnt at home.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For nothing, nothing is more powerful than a pious and sensible women to bring a man into proper order, and to mould his soul as she will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he will not endure friends, or teachers, or rulers, as he will his partner advising and counseling him, since the advice carries even some pleasure with it, because she who gives the counsel is greatly loved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I could tell of many hard and disobedient men who have been softened in this way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For she who shares his table, his bed, and his embraces, his words and secrets, his comings in and goings out, and many other things, who is entirely given up and joined to him, as it is likely that a body would be joined to a head, if she happen to be discreet and well attuned, will go beyond and excel all others in the management of her husband.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore I exhort women to make this their employment, and to give fitting counsel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as they have great power for good, so have they also for evil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A women destroyed Absalom, a woman destroyed Amnon, a woman was like to have destroyed Job, a woman rescued Nabal from the slaughter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Women have preserved whole nations; for Deborah and Judith exhibited successes worthy of men; so also do ten thousand other women.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul saith, “For what knowest thou, O wife, whether thou shalt save thy husband?” ( 1 Cor. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in those times we see Persis and Mary and Priscilla taking part in the labors of the Apostles ( Rom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 ); whom we also needs must imitate, and not by words only, but also by actions, bring into order him that dwelleth with us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how shall we instruct him by our actions?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When he sees that thou art not evilly disposed, not fond of expense or ornament, not demanding extravagant supplies of money, but content with what thou hast, then will he endure thee counseling him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if thou art wise in word, and in actions doest the contrary, he will condemn thee for very foolish talking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when together with words thou affordest him also instruction by thy works, then will he admit thee and obey thee the more readily; as when thou desirest not gold, nor pearls, nor costly clothing, but instead of these, modesty, sobriety, kindness; when thou exhibitest these virtues on thy part and requirest them on his.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if thou must needs do somewhat to please thy husband, thou shouldest adorn thy soul, not adorn and so spoil thy person.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The gold which thou puttest about thee will not make thee so lovely and desirable to him, as modesty and kindness towards himself, and a readiness to die for thy partner; these things most subdue men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed, that splendor of apparel even displeases him, as straitening his means, and causing him much expense and care; but those things which I have named will rivet a husband to a wife; for kindness and friendship and love cause no cares, give rise to no expense, but quite the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That outward adornment becomes palling by use, but that of the soul blooms day by day, and kindles a stronger flame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that if thou wouldest please thy husband, adorn thy soul with modesty, piety, and management of the house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things both subdue him more, and never cease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Age destroys not this adornment, sickness wastes it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The adornment of the body length of time is wont to undo, sickness and many other things to waste, but what relates to the soul is above all this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That adornment causes envy, and kindles jealousy, but this is pure from disease, and free from all vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus will matters at home be easier, and your income without trouble, when the gold is not laid on about your body or encircling your arms, but passes on to necessary uses, such as the feeding of servants, the necessary care of children, and other useful purposes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if this be not the case, if the (wife’s) face be covered with ornaments, while the (husband’s) heart is pressed by anxiety, what profit, what kind of advantage is there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The one being grieved allows not the marvelous beauty of the other to be seen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For ye know, ye know that though a man see the most beautiful of all women, he cannot feel pleasure at the sight while his soul is sorrowful, because in order to feel pleasure a man must first rejoice and be glad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And when all his gold is heaped together to adorn a woman’s body, while there is distress in his dwelling, her partner can have no pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that if we desire to be agreeable to our husbands, let us give them pleasure; and we shall give them pleasure, if we remove our ornaments and fineries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For all these things at the actual time of marriage appear to afford some delight, but this afterwards fades by time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if when the heaven is so beautiful, and the sun, to which thou canst not name any body that is equal, so bright, we admire them less from habitually seeing them, how shall we admire a body tricked out with gewgaws?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things I say, desiring that you should be adorned with that wholesome adornment which Paul enjoined; “Not with gold, or pearls, or costly array; but (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works.” ( 1 Tim. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But dost thou wish to please strangers, and to be praised by them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then assuredly this is not the desire of a modest woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: However, if thou wishest it, by doing as I have said, thou wilt have strangers also to love thee much, and to praise thy modesty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the woman who adorns her person no virtuous and sober person will praise, but the intemperate and lascivious; nay, rather neither will these praise her, but will even speak vilely of her, having their eyes inflamed by the wantonness displayed about her; but the other all will approve, both the one sort and the other, because they receive no harm from her, but even instruction in heavenly wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And great shall be her praise from men, and great her reward with God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: After such adornment then let us strive, that we may live here without fear, and may obtain the blessings which are to come; which may we all obtain through the grace and loving-kindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
